program.name: BHP Graduate Program (Australia)
program.salary_text: The job ad says the program offers a competitive salary package, plus a performance-based bonus and an employee share program.
program.duration_text: The program is described as a 24-month graduate program.
program.rotation_text: Graduates will rotate into different teams 2-4 times during the program.

eligibility.work_rights: International students with a valid visa are welcome to apply. By February 2027, you must hold a visa with no work restrictions.
eligibility.graduation_window: You must have graduated within the past two years, or be due to graduate by the end of 2026.

stages.name: Stage 1: Application
stages.details: Apply online and include your resume. BHP recommends checking your resume for typos and double-checking all application responses and contact details. BHP notes that a cover letter and academic transcript are not required at this stage.

stages.name: Stage 2: Online assessment
stages.details: BHP describes this as a video interview and cognitive testing, and recommends following the email instructions, choosing a quiet place, and ensuring your device is charged with a strong internet connection.

stages.name: Stage 3: 1-on-1 conversation with a line leader
stages.details: BHP recommends finding a quiet place if by phone/video, letting BHP know if you cannot make the time so it can be rescheduled, reviewing your resume, and familiarising yourself with BHP’s values and purpose.

stages.name: Stage 6: Provide your academic transcript prior to your start date (graduates only)

tips: Talk to BHP employees to learn more about the company.
Understand the BHP Charter Values and what they mean for you.
Do the practice tests for the online cognitive testing.
Treat the video interview as a conversation and answer naturally.
If you need modifications to the testing process due to your situation, submit a query via bhp.com/contact.

exec_themes: Run “tier 1” mining assets safely and efficiently, with a strong focus on operational excellence and productivity.
Use disciplined capital allocation (Capital Allocation Framework) to balance growth investment and shareholder returns (dividends) through commodity cycles.
Grow production in key commodities (notably copper and iron ore), supported by ongoing investment and a resilient portfolio.
Progress decarbonisation plans for operational emissions and work with suppliers/customers on value-chain emissions where possible.
Build “social value” (e.g., Indigenous procurement, workforce inclusion) as a way to keep operations stable, reduce risk, and access opportunities and capital.
commercial_context.profit_engine: BHP’s profit engine is high-volume, low-cost production from large, long-life assets (notably being the world’s lowest-cost major iron ore producer at WAIO), combined with operational excellence, cost discipline, and disciplined capital allocation that supports strong margins and cash generation used for dividends and growth capex.
commercial_context.headwinds: BHP highlights a volatile/uncertain external environment, commodity-market volatility, and inflation pressures (especially higher labour costs). On the climate side, BHP notes that some decarbonisation technologies needed for its long-term net zero goal are not yet ready and that the pace of development for some technologies has slowed, creating delays/updates to parts of its transition pathway.
commercial_context.esg: BHP frames ESG as central to long-term value creation: safety as the top priority; reducing operational (Scope 1 and 2) greenhouse gas emissions (including being on track for at least a 30% reduction from FY2020 levels by FY2030, and reporting a reduction in FY2025 versus FY2024); publishing and seeking shareholder support for its Climate Transition Action Plan; and social value initiatives such as Indigenous procurement and progress on workforce gender balance.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: BHP’s most explicit recent shift is an updated/second Climate Transition Action Plan (CTAP 2024, published August 2024) and subsequent updates to parts of its long-term decarbonisation pathway due to technology delays identified through planning since CTAP 2024. It is also emphasizing growth investment plans (including Jansen potash, copper growth options) while maintaining disciplined capital allocation and dividends.

notes: Program duration and rotations were taken from the Australia graduate and student programs page.
Closing date and visa/work rights requirements were taken from the careers.bhp.com job ad for the 2027 Australian Graduate Campaign.
The BHP page states applications are open but does not give a specific opening date, so open_date is left null.

notes: This summary is limited to what is explicitly stated on BHP’s official pages listed in the sources. Some items (e.g., ‘pivot’) are inferred as ‘recently emphasized change’ based on BHP describing CTAP 2024 and subsequent updates; BHP does not label these as a ‘pivot’ in the cited text.

The sources used include BHP’s Annual Report webpage(s), a FY2025 results media release, and BHP’s Climate Transition Action Plan page, which are official but not full transcripts in the extracted snippets here.
